books.title: Cosmos
books.author: Carl Sagan
books.quote: The cosmos is within us. We are made of star-stuff. We are a way for the universe to know itself.
books.note: The book that made me look up. Sagan writes about the universe the way a poet writes about love — with reverence, wonder, and an ache for what we don't yet understand. Every chapter is a door into something vast.

books.title: The Camera
books.author: Ansel Adams
books.quote: You don't take a photograph, you make it.
books.note: Adams treats the camera not as a gadget but as an extension of vision. Reading this while learning photography changed how I see light, shadow, and the patience a single frame demands.

books.title: Zen and the Art of Motorcycle Maintenance
books.author: Robert M. Pirsig
books.quote: The only Zen you find on the tops of mountains is the Zen you bring up there.
books.note: Part road trip, part philosophy lecture, part quiet breakdown. Pirsig asks what quality really means, and by the end you realize the question matters more than any answer.

books.title: Gödel, Escher, Bach
books.author: Douglas Hofstadter
books.quote: In the end, we are self-perceiving, self-inventing, locked-in mirages that are little miracles of self-reference.
books.note: A book about loops, recursion, and the strange way consciousness emerges from matter. It sits on the shelf waiting — I know once I start, everything else stops.
